claim_scope: finite proof-carrying claim record envelope only
interpretation_mapping: The natural-language claim is narrowed to the finite record predicate checked by the local Lean module.
formal_scope: Finite record fields for tier, interpretation mapping, artifact refs, limitations, support-state effect, and non-claims only; no general theorem validity is claimed.
consumer_requirements: treat as eligible for bounded evidence review only
preserve the formal scope when cited
keep chapter support state unchanged unless an accepted evidence transition exists
downgrade_rule: If the Lean build fails or the formal scope is broadened, block promotion and route to proof adequacy review.
residual_route: Record any broader semantic, source, or verifier-quality claim as a residual.
ledger_update: Eligible for bounded evidence review of the finite record envelope only; no support-state promotion is applied.
non_claims: does not promote support state without accepted evidence transition
